title: Tech
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Tech, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: OpenAI taps Tata for 100MW AI data center capacity in India, eyes 1GW
items.description: OpenAI also plans to expand its presence in India with new offices in Mumbai and Bengaluru later this year.

items.title: OpenAI deepens India push with Pine Labs fintech partnership
items.description: OpenAI moves beyond ChatGPT in India with a Pine Labs deal targeting enterprise payments and AI-driven commerce.

items.title: Hacking conference Def Con bans three people linked to Epstein

items.title: SeatGeek and Spotify team up to offer concert ticket sales inside the music platform
items.description: SeatGeek announced on Wednesday a new integration with Spotify, bringing a seamless ticket-buying experience for Spotify users. Now, when Spotify user...

items.title: Is your startup’s check engine light on? Google Cloud’s VP explains what to do
items.description: Startup founders are being pushed to move faster than ever, using AI while facing tighter funding, rising infrastructure costs, and more pressure to s...

items.title: Etsy sells secondhand clothing marketplace Depop to eBay for $1.2B
items.description: The deal comes nearly five years after Etsy purchased Depop for $1.62 billion, at a time when secondhand clothing apps were gaining traction during th...

items.title: Nvidia’s Deal With Meta Signals a New Era in Computing Power
items.description: The days of tech giants buying up discrete chips are over. AI companies now need GPUs, CPUs, and everything in between.

items.title: Save on Gaming Earbuds Perfect for Playing on Switch or ROG Xbox Ally
items.description: While it may sound unorthodox, these earbuds make a great companion for your Switch 2 or other portable console.

items.title: This Defense Company Made AI Agents That Blow Things Up
items.description: Scout AI is using technology borrowed from the AI industry to power lethal weapons—and recently demonstrated its explosive potential.

items.title: A startup called Germ becomes the first private messenger that launches directly from Bluesky’s app
items.description: Social network Bluesky now offers private messaging by integrating the startup Germ's E2E encrypted messenger natively in its app.
